title: MFFC — the "private cone" of a node

steps.title: Two outputs (t, g); n is shared between them
steps.narrative:
An MFFC w.r.t. a cut is the set of internal ANDs that would die if the target were replaced — only counting nodes above the cut whose only consumer (transitively) is the target.

This AIG has two outputs: t (target) and g. n is consumed by both (look at n's [2]). So even though n is in t's cone, n is not private to t — it must survive any replacement of t. The walk needs to detect this and reject n.

Cut for t: {p, q, w, z, e, h}. p, q, w, z are internal ANDs (fanout 1 into the cone); e, h are PIs (fanout 1 into the cone).

entries.p, q, w, z: internal ANDs at the cut, each fanout [1]
entries.n: e · h (fanout [2] — feeds t AND g)

panes.title: Expected MFFC
panes.body: { t, m, u, v } — 4 of 9 internal ANDs. n is rejected (sharing). p, q, w, z are protected by the boundary boost. e, h, y, g, PO_g are never touched.

steps.title: Fanout counters and the two protections
steps.narrative:
Each AND carries a live fanout count (the [N] xlabels). The walk dereferences t top-down, decrementing each visited node's count by 1. If a count reaches zero the node has no remaining consumer outside t's cone → mark it and recurse into its fanins. Otherwise stop.

Two failure modes a naive walk has to handle:

(a) Cut leaves p, q, w, z have fanout 1. They feed only into t's cone. Decrement → 0 → the walk would recurse past them into a, b, c, d, w1, w2, z1, z2, which live below the cut. Fix: +1 boost on every cut leaf (undone after).

(b) Shared in-cone node n has fanout 2 — it feeds both t and the unrelated output g. Decrement leaves it at 1, not zero, and the walk stops there by itself. No fix needed: sharing is natural protection.

panes.title: How each non-MFFC node escapes the walk
rows: yes — would walk past the cut

steps.title: Apply boundary boost: +1 on every cut leaf
steps.narrative: abcRewrite.c:138..148 brackets the deref with a +1/-1 on each cut-leaf's fanout count. Boost only matters for cut leaves with fanout 1; for cut leaves that already have multiple consumers it's redundant (but harmless). Watch the six xlabels jump from [1] to [2]:

steps.title: Deref t → count = 1, mark t
steps.narrative: deref(t): count = 1, label t with current TravId. Walk into fanins m and n.

steps.title: Deref m: 1 → 0 → recurse, count = 2
steps.narrative: Decrement m.fanouts: 1 → 0 → recurse, mark m. Walk continues into m's fanins u and v.

steps.title: Deref u (m's left fanin): 1 → 0 → recurse, count = 3; boost stops at p, w
steps.narrative:
u.fanouts: 1 → 0 → recurse, mark u. count = 3.

Now walk into u's fanins p and w. Both are cut leaves at boosted count 2:
deref(p): 2 → 1, NOT zero → STOP. p stays out.
deref(w): 2 → 1, NOT zero → STOP. w stays out.

Without the boost, p and w would have hit zero here and we'd have walked into a, b, w1, w2 — the wrong outcome.

steps.title: Deref v (m's right fanin): 1 → 0 → recurse, count = 4; boost stops at q, z
steps.narrative: v.fanouts: 1 → 0 → recurse, mark v. count = 4. Walk into q and z; same boost protection — both 2 → 1, recursion stops.

steps.title: Sharing rejects n: 2 → 1, NOT zero — n NOT in MFFC
steps.narrative:
Back at t, deref the second fanin n. n's fanout count is 2 (it feeds t AND g). Decrement → 1. Not zero → recursion STOPS, n is not added to the MFFC.

This is a different rejection mechanism from the boost: n is excluded because it has a real external consumer (g). The deref leaves n alive, exactly as it should — any replacement of t will reuse n's output via the existing edge.

Note: e, h were never decremented — the recursion didn't enter n.

panes.body: Boost vs. sharing — both stop deref recursion. Boost is artificial protection ABC adds for cut leaves; sharing is real Boolean-network protection. Both yield the same effect: count doesn't reach zero, no recursion, node not in MFFC.

steps.title: Result: MFFC = {t, m, u, v} — 4 of 9 internal ANDs
steps.narrative: Recursion done. count = 4. The 4 red nodes are exactly the ANDs private to t. The 6 cut leaves (blue, dashed) survive via boost. n survives via sharing — its fanout to g protected it without any artificial boost. g, y, PO_g and everything below the cut is entirely untouched.

entries.Cut leaves (blue): p, q, w, z, e, h — via boost
entries.Untouched (gray): y, g, PO_g, plus everything below the cut

panes.title: Re-reference pass
panes.body: Abc_NodeMffcLabelAig follows up with a re-ref pass walking the same red path in reverse, restoring every fanout count. Then the +1 boost on cut leaves is stripped. The AIG is byte-identical to its original state — only the red nodes carry the current TravId.
